meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching: Finish Your AI Projects as a Student
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your code. Learn how to use freelance pitching to force shipping, using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio and career.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle ends when you introduce external accountability. By pitching a real client, you shift from 'hobbyist playing with code' to 'developer delivering value.' Use Antigravity to scaffold your MVP and Claude Code to automate the implementation, ensuring you ship a production-ready feature before the deadline.
content.intro:
Your desktop is a graveyard of half-baked repositories and 'Hello World' clones. You have the curiosity, but you lack the gravity—that crushing force of a deadline that separates the dreamers from the engineers. The dopamine hit of starting a new project is blinding you to the reality of the industry: code is worthless until it is deployed.

Freelance pitching isn't just about money; it’s a high-stakes forcing function. When you pitch a real business owner, the abstract fear of 'not finishing' transforms into a concrete obligation. This guide shows you how to leverage modern AI tools to turn that pressure into a finished, portfolio-grade asset that proves you can build, break, and ship.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Scope
steps.description: Don't over-engineer. Find a business problem that can be solved in 7 days. Use this phase to outline the MVP features that provide immediate value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business problem: [Insert Problem]. Suggest a 7-day MVP scope that solves the core pain point without feature creep.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project requirements document for a 7-day MVP based on [Business Goal].'

steps.title: Architect the Scaffold
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular architecture. This prevents the 'spaghetti code' that usually leads to abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a SaaS app using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include a modular directory structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'Happy Path'. Use Claude Code to iterate on the primary business logic while you focus on the UI/UX.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the logic for the primary business function: [Insert Logic]. Use clean, testable TypeScript code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core business logic in /src/services and add unit tests for each function.'

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Never manually test your entire app. Use AI to write tests that run every time you push code, ensuring you don't break the build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate integration tests for the authentication and primary user flow using Vitest.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute integration tests for the primary user flow.'

steps.title: Deployment and Client Handoff
steps.description: The final step. Deploy to Vercel or Railway and prepare the documentation for your client. This is the moment you officially 'finish'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise README and a client handoff guide for this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure deployment script for Vercel and generate a professional README.md.'

pro_tips: Always charge a small fee, even for your first project; money creates a psychological contract that prevents ghosting.
Use a 'Feature Freeze' date—tell your client no new features after day 5 to ensure you hit the deadline.
Leverage Claude Code's memory to keep your project documentation updated in real-time as you code.
Build 'in public' by sharing your daily progress on LinkedIn; the social pressure is a powerful antidote to procrastination.
Focus on the 'Time-to-Value' for the client rather than the 'Time-to-Code' for yourself.
content.next_steps: After completing your first client project, take the codebase and refactor it into a reusable template. Use this template to pitch three more clients in the same niche to scale your freelance income.
content.conversion_block: You are only one project away from building the career you want. Stop letting your ideas die in a private repository. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
